SUMMARY: NESTED | FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD DETAIL: FIELD | CONSTR | METHOD

javax.media.mscontrol.mediagroup
Interface RecorderEvent

All Superinterfaces:
MediaEvent<Recorder>, ResourceEvent<Recorder>, SpeechDetectorConstants
public interface RecorderEvent
extends ResourceEvent<Recorder>, SpeechDetectorConstants

Defines the methods used in Recorder events.

Recorder events include when an RTC triggers a pause or resume.

Field Summary
static Qualifier DURATION_EXCEEDED
          Record ended because the maximum record duration time has been reached.
static EventType PAUSED
          Indicates that recording has been paused.
static Qualifier PROMPT_FAILURE
          Returned by RecorderEvent.getQualifier() to indicate that the record operation was aborted because the prompt could not be played.
static EventType RECORD_COMPLETED
          Indicates that recording has been completed.
static EventType RESUMED
          Indicates that recording has been resumed.
static Qualifier SILENCE
          Value for RecorderEvent.getQualifier().
static EventType STARTED
          Indicates that the recording has started.
 
Fields inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.resource.ResourceEvent
MANUAL_TRIGGER, NO_QUALIFIER, RTC_TRIGGERED, STOPPED
 
Fields inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.MediaEvent
NO_ERROR

 int getDuration()
          Returns the length of the recording, in milliseconds.

Methods inherited from interface javax.media.mscontrol.MediaEvent

Field Detail

RECORD_COMPLETED

static final EventType RECORD_COMPLETED
Indicates that recording has been completed. Possible qualifiers are: ResourceEvent.RTC_TRIGGERED, ResourceEvent.STOPPED, DURATION_EXCEEDED, SILENCE.

PAUSED

static final EventType PAUSED
Indicates that recording has been paused.

RESUMED

static final EventType RESUMED
Indicates that recording has been resumed.

STARTED

static final EventType STARTED
Indicates that the recording has started. A video recorder may wait for the reception of a stream synchronization point, and this takes a noticeable amount of time.
Note: This event must be enabled, see Recorder.ENABLED_EVENTS

DURATION_EXCEEDED

static final Qualifier DURATION_EXCEEDED
Record ended because the maximum record duration time has been reached.

SILENCE

static final Qualifier SILENCE
Value for RecorderEvent.getQualifier(). Indicates that the record was terminated because silence was detected.

PROMPT_FAILURE

static final Qualifier PROMPT_FAILURE
Returned by RecorderEvent.getQualifier() to indicate that the record operation was aborted because the prompt could not be played.
See getError() and getErrorText() for more.

Method Detail

getDuration

int getDuration()
Returns the length of the recording, in milliseconds. This length does not include any omitted silence.
